title: centaurea maculosa subsp. corymbosa
intro: Centaurea maculosa subsp. corymbosa is a subspecies of knapweed, a perennial herb in the sunflower family, characterized by its distinctive flower clusters and taproot. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea maculosa subsp. corymbosa include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
